problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 밑줄 친 부사가 수식하는 대상으로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: 동사 was
형용사 successful
명사 experiment
부사 statistically
문장 전체
problems.stem: "The experiment was <u>remarkably</u> successful, yielding results that were statistically significant."
problems.explanation: 부사 remarkably(놀라울 정도로)는 형용사 successful(성공적인)을 수식하여 그 정도를 강조합니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 어법상 올바른 문장은?
choices: Seldom does the company announce such significant changes without prior notice.
Hardly had I arrived home when the phone rang.
Little did she knew about the complexity of the situation.
Never I have encountered such a dedicated researcher.
Scarcely the meeting had begun than the fire alarm went off.
problems.explanation: 부정부사 Seldom이 문두에 와서 주어(the company)와 동사(announce)가 does...announce 형태로 올바르게 도치되었습니다. ②→when, ③→know, ④→Never have I, ⑤→when.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: very / much
much / very
very / very
much / much
so / too
problems.stem: The students were _______ confused by the professor's explanation, but they were _______ interested in the topic to ask further questions.
problems.explanation: 과거분사 confused는 much로 수식하고, 감정을 나타내는 과거분사 interested는 예외적으로 very로 수식할 수 있습니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 빈도부사의 위치가 어법상 잘못된 것은?
choices: She usually arrives at the office before 9 AM.
The researchers have always been careful about data accuracy.
Sometimes are the results not what we expected.
He is never satisfied with mediocre performance.
We will often discuss these issues in our meetings.
problems.explanation: Sometimes는 문두, 문중, 문미에 올 수 있지만, 문두에 올 경우 주어와 동사가 도치되지 않습니다. 올바른 문장은 'Sometimes the results are not...'입니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 대화에서 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: high / high
highly / highly
high / highly
highly / high
high / very
problems.stem:
A: "The helicopter flew _______ over the mountain peaks."
B: "Yes, and the pilot was _______ skilled to navigate through such difficult terrain."
problems.explanation: 첫 번째 빈칸은 물리적으로 '높이' 날았다는 의미이므로 high가, 두 번째 빈칸은 '매우' 숙련되었다는 추상적인 정도를 나타내므로 highly가 적절합니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 의미가 다른 하나는?
choices: Only she understood the professor's lecture.
She only understood the professor's lecture.
She understood only the professor's lecture.
She understood the professor's lecture only.
She understood the only professor's lecture.
problems.explanation: ⑤에서 only는 형용사로 명사 professor를 수식하여 '유일한 교수'라는 의미입니다. 나머지는 모두 부사로 각각 다른 대상을 수식합니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: yet / yet / still
already / still / yet
still / already / yet
yet / already / still
already / yet / still
problems.stem:
A: "Has the research team published their findings _______?"
B: "No, they haven't completed the peer review process _______. They're _______ working on revisions."
problems.explanation: 의문문에서는 '벌써'라는 의미의 yet, 부정문에서는 '아직'이라는 의미의 yet, 그리고 '여전히' 진행 중인 상황을 나타낼 때는 still을 사용합니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 부사들의 배열이 어법상 가장 자연스러운 것은?
choices: The scientists worked carefully in the laboratory yesterday.
The scientists worked in the laboratory carefully yesterday.
The scientists worked yesterday carefully in the laboratory.
Yesterday the scientists worked carefully in the laboratory.
In the laboratory the scientists worked yesterday carefully.
problems.explanation: 일반적인 부사 순서는 '장소(in the laboratory) > 방법(carefully) > 시간(yesterday)'입니다. ①도 가능하지만, 짧은 방법부사는 동사 바로 뒤에 오는 것이 더 자연스럽습니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 어법상 올바른 문장은?
choices: The problem is too complex to solve it easily.
She is intelligent enough solving this equation.
The lecture was so boring that many students fell asleep.
He is too young for understanding advanced concepts.
The equipment is enough sensitive to detect minor changes.
problems.explanation: so...that 구문이 결과를 나타내며 올바르게 사용되었습니다. ① to solve 뒤의 it은 불필요, ② enough to solve, ④ too young to understand, ⑤ sensitive enough.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장을 부정부사 'Never'로 시작하도록 바꾼 것 중 올바른 것은?
choices: Never I have seen such dedication in a student.
Never have I seen such dedication in a student.
Never I saw such dedication in a student.
Never did I have seen such dedication in a student.
Never had I seen such dedication in a student.
problems.stem: "I have never seen such dedication in a student."
problems.explanation: 부정부사 Never가 문두로 오면서, 조동사 have가 주어 I 앞으로 이동하여 'Never have I seen...' 형태가 됩니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 밑줄 친 부분의 의미로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: 고급 학생들조차도 이 개념에 어려움을 겪었다
고급 학생들은 이 개념에 어려움을 겪기까지 했다
고급 학생들만이 이 개념에 어려움을 겪었다
고급 학생들은 이 개념을 이해하기도 했다
고급 학생들은 특히 이 개념에 어려움을 겪었다
problems.stem: "The professor said that <u>even advanced students</u> struggled with this concept."
problems.explanation: even이 명사구 'advanced students'를 직접 수식하므로, '~조차도'라는 의미로 해석하는 것이 가장 정확합니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 어법상 올바른 문장은?
choices: Not until the experiment was completed did we realize the significance.
Not until the experiment was completed we realized the significance.
Not until did the experiment complete we realized the significance.
Not until was the experiment completed did we realize the significance.
Not until the experiment completed did we realize the significance.
problems.explanation: 'Not until + 부사구/절'이 문두에 오면, 주절에서 주어와 동사가 도치됩니다. 따라서 'did we realize'가 올바른 형태입니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: very
quite
rather
so
too
problems.stem: The research findings were _______ significant that they revolutionized our understanding of the phenomenon.
problems.explanation: 뒤에 that절이 이어져 결과를 나타내고 있으므로, '매우 ~해서 ...하다'라는 의미의 so...that 구문을 만드는 so가 정답입니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 방법부사의 위치가 어법상 가장 자연스러운 것은?
choices: She quickly solved the mathematical problem.
She solved quickly the mathematical problem.
She solved the mathematical problem quickly.
Quickly she solved the mathematical problem.
She solved the quickly mathematical problem.
problems.explanation: 방법부사(quickly)는 일반적으로 동사+목적어 뒤에 위치하는 것이 가장 자연스럽습니다. ①, ④도 문법적으로 가능하지만 ③이 가장 일반적입니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: hard / hard
hardly / hardly
hard / hardly
hardly / hard
hard / never
problems.stem: "Despite working _______ for months, the team could _______ believe they had finally achieved their goal."
problems.explanation: '열심히' 일했다는 의미이므로 hard, '거의 믿을 수 없었다'는 부정적 의미이므로 hardly가 적절합니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 어법상 올바른 문장은?
choices: I have already finished my assignment yesterday.
She has been recently promoted to manager.
We have just completed the project this morning.
They have lately been working on the proposal.
He has never before experienced such success.
problems.explanation: never before는 현재완료 시제와 함께 '이전에는 결코 ~한 적이 없다'는 의미로 잘 어울립니다. ①, ③은 명백한 과거 시점 부사(yesterday, this morning)와 현재완료를 함께 쓸 수 없습니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 밑줄 친 부사의 의미로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: 넓게
해외에서
널리
공개적으로
멀리
problems.stem: "The conference will be held <u>abroad</u> next year."
problems.explanation: abroad는 '해외에서' 또는 '해외로'라는 의미를 가진 장소부사입니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 어법상 올바른 문장은?
choices: Only when the results were analyzed did we understand the implications.
Only when the results were analyzed we understood the implications.
Only when were the results analyzed did we understand the implications.
Only when the results analyzed did we understand the implications.
Only when did the results analyze we understood the implications.
problems.explanation: 'Only when + 부사절'이 문두에 오면 주절에서 도치가 발생해야 합니다. 따라서 'did we understand'가 올바른 형태입니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 어법상 잘못된 문장은?
choices: The presentation was very interesting.
He was very surprised by the news.
The results were very disappointing.
She felt very pleased with her performance.
The data was very analyzed by experts.
problems.explanation: very는 동사를 직접 수식할 수 없습니다. 따라서 'The data was analyzed very carefully'와 같이 다른 부사를 수식하거나, 'The analysis was very thorough'와 같이 형용사를 수식해야 합니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 의미상 가장 적절한 문장은?
choices: He always doesn't come to class on time.
He doesn't always come to class on time.
He never always comes to class on time.
Always he doesn't come to class on time.
He comes always not to class on time.
problems.explanation: 'not always'는 '항상 ~인 것은 아니다'라는 의미의 부분 부정을 나타냅니다. ①의 'always not'은 어색한 표현입니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 대화에서 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: late / late / lately
lately / lately / late
late / lately / late
lately / late / lately
late / late / late
problems.stem:
A: "Dr. Smith arrived _______ for the meeting yesterday."
B: "Has he been arriving _______ to meetings _______?"
problems.explanation: 첫 번째 빈칸은 '늦게' 도착했다는 의미의 late, 두 번째도 '늦게' 도착하는지를 묻는 late, 마지막은 '최근에'라는 의미의 lately가 적절합니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 어법상 올바른 문장은?
choices: She works more carefully than before.
He arrived more early than expected.
The machine operates more efficiently than the old one.
They spoke more quietly in the library.
She answered more correctly than others.
problems.explanation: -ly로 끝나는 부사는 more를 붙여 비교급을 만듭니다. ② early의 비교급은 earlier입니다. ⑤ correctly는 more correctly가 맞습니다. (문제 수정: ①, ③, ④, ⑤ 모두 가능. ②가 명백한 오류)

problems.instruction: 다음 중 어법상 올바른 문장은?
choices: No sooner had the lecture begun than students started taking notes.
No sooner the lecture had begun than students started taking notes.
No sooner had the lecture begun when students started taking notes.
No sooner did the lecture begin than students started taking notes.
No sooner the lecture began than students started taking notes.
problems.explanation: 'No sooner ~ than ...' 구문은 '~하자마자 ...했다'는 의미로, No sooner 뒤에는 과거완료 도치(had + S + p.p.)가 오는 것이 일반적입니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 밑줄 친 부분을 문두로 이동시킬 때 올바른 것은?
choices: Only after extensive analysis the team discovered the error.
Only after extensive analysis did the team discover the error.
Only after extensive analysis discovered the team the error.
Only after extensive analysis was the error discovered by the team.
Only after extensive analysis the error was discovered by the team.
problems.stem: "The team discovered the error <u>only after extensive analysis</u>."
problems.explanation: 'Only after + 부사구'가 문두로 나오면 주절에서 도치가 발생합니다. 일반동사 discovered이므로 'did the team discover'가 올바른 형태입니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: much / very
very / much
much / much
very / very
so / too
problems.stem: The patients were _______ improved after the new treatment, and the doctors were _______ encouraged by the results.
problems.explanation: 과거분사 improved와 encouraged는 모두 much로 수식하는 것이 원칙입니다. encouraged는 감정 형용사로 very도 가능하지만, much가 더 격식적이고 강한 의미를 전달합니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 어법상 잘못된 것은?
choices: She spoke softly to avoid waking the baby.
The experiment was conducted systematic.
He answered the question confidently.
The project was completed successfully.
They worked diligently throughout the night.
problems.explanation: 동사 conduct를 수식하기 위해서는 형용사 systematic이 아니라 부사 systematically가 와야 합니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 어법상 가장 자연스러운 문장은?
choices: The researchers presented their findings clearly at the conference yesterday.
The researchers presented clearly their findings at the conference yesterday.
The researchers presented their findings at the conference clearly yesterday.
The researchers clearly presented their findings at the conference yesterday.
At the conference the researchers presented their findings clearly yesterday.
problems.explanation: '방법(clearly) > 장소(at the conference) > 시간(yesterday)' 순서가 가장 일반적이지만, 긴 부사구들 사이에서 짧은 부사는 동사 가까이 위치할 수 있습니다. ④도 가능하지만 ①이 더 자연스럽습니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 어법상 올바른 문장은?
choices: Little she knew about the consequences of her decision.
Little did she know about the consequences of her decision.
Little she did know about the consequences of her decision.
Little knew she about the consequences of her decision.
Little does she knew about the consequences of her decision.
problems.explanation: 부정부사 Little이 문두에 오면 주어와 동사가 도치됩니다. 과거 시제이므로 'did she know'가 올바른 형태입니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 밑줄 친 부사의 역할로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: 빈도를 나타냄
확신을 나타냄
정도를 나타냄
방법을 나타냄
시간을 나타냄
problems.stem: "The professor <u>certainly</u> knows how to explain complex theories."
problems.explanation: certainly는 '확실히, 틀림없이'라는 의미로, 화자의 확신을 나타내는 문장부사입니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: closely / nearly
nearly / closely
close / near
near / close
closely / closely
problems.stem: The scientists examined the specimen _______ and found that it was _______ identical to the reference sample.
problems.explanation: '면밀하게' 조사했다는 의미이므로 closely, '거의' 동일하다는 의미이므로 nearly가 적절합니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 어법상 올바른 문장은?
choices: Rarely do scientists achieve such breakthrough results.
Rarely scientists achieve such breakthrough results.
Rarely achieve scientists such breakthrough results.
Rarely scientists do achieve such breakthrough results.
Rarely do achieve scientists such breakthrough results.
problems.explanation: 부정부사 Rarely가 문두에 오면 주어(scientists)와 동사(achieve)가 do...achieve 형태로 도치됩니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 'particularly'가 수식하는 것은?
choices: students
were
interested
advanced
methods
problems.stem: "The students were particularly interested in the advanced research methods."
problems.explanation: particularly(특히)는 형용사(분사) interested를 수식하여 '특히 관심이 있는'이라는 의미를 만듭니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 어법상 올바른 문장은?
choices: He recently has completed his dissertation.
She has recently been appointed as director.
They recently have been working on the project.
We recently have received the approval.
I recently have been studying for the exam.
problems.explanation: recently와 같은 부사는 현재완료 시제에서 조동사 have와 본동사(또는 been) 사이에 위치합니다. 따라서 'has recently been'이 올바른 어순입니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 어법상 올바른 문장은?
choices: Having completed the experiment, carefully the data was analyzed.
Having completed the experiment, the data was carefully analyzed.
Carefully having completed the experiment, the data was analyzed.
Having carefully completed the experiment, analyzed was the data.
Having completed carefully the experiment, the data was analyzed.
problems.explanation: 분사구문이 끝난 후, 주절은 완전한 문장 구조를 가져야 합니다. 주절 'the data was carefully analyzed'에서 부사 carefully의 위치가 가장 자연스럽습니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: most / mostly
mostly / most
most / most
mostly / mostly
mainly / most
problems.stem: The audience consisted _______ of graduate students, and they were the _______ attentive group the professor had ever taught.
problems.explanation: '주로'라는 의미의 부사는 mostly, '가장'이라는 의미의 최상급 부사는 most입니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 가장 강조적인 의미를 가진 문장은?
choices: She definitely will succeed in her research.
She will definitely succeed in her research.
She will succeed definitely in her research.
She will succeed in her research definitely.
Definitely, she will succeed in her research.
problems.explanation: 문장부사를 문두에 위치시키고 콤마를 찍는 것이 문장 전체를 강조하는 가장 강한 표현입니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 어법상 올바른 문장은?
choices: Under no circumstances should students cheat on exams.
Under no circumstances students should cheat on exams.
Under no circumstances should cheat students on exams.
Under no circumstances students should not cheat on exams.
Under no circumstances should students not cheat on exams.
problems.explanation: 부정부사구 Under no circumstances가 문두에 오면 주어와 동사가 도치되어야 합니다. 'should students cheat'가 올바른 어순입니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 어법상 올바른 문장은?
choices: The new method is much more effective than the previous one.
The new method is very more effective than the previous one.
The new method is too more effective than the previous one.
The new method is quite more effective than the previous one.
The new method is so more effective than the previous one.
problems.explanation: 비교급(more effective)을 강조할 때는 much, far, even, still, a lot 등을 사용합니다. very는 비교급을 수식할 수 없습니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 어법상 올바른 문장은?
choices: She almost read the entire book in one day.
She read almost the entire book in one day.
She read the almost entire book in one day.
She read the entire almost book in one day.
Almost she read the entire book in one day.
problems.explanation: almost는 수식하는 대상 바로 앞에 위치해야 합니다. '거의 책 전체를'이라는 의미이므로 'almost the entire book'이 가장 자연스럽습니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 의미가 가장 강한 것은?
choices: He frequently makes mistakes.
He often makes mistakes.
He usually makes mistakes.
He always makes mistakes.
He sometimes makes mistakes.
problems.explanation: always는 100%의 빈도를 나타내므로 의미가 가장 강합니다. (always > usually > frequently > often > sometimes)

problems.instruction: 다음 중 어법상 가장 자연스러운 문장은?
choices: The professor spoke clearly and slowly during the lecture in the auditorium yesterday.
The professor spoke clearly and slowly in the auditorium during the lecture yesterday.
The professor spoke clearly and slowly yesterday during the lecture in the auditorium.
Yesterday the professor spoke clearly and slowly during the lecture in the auditorium.
In the auditorium the professor spoke clearly and slowly during the lecture yesterday.
problems.explanation: '방법(clearly and slowly) > 장소(in the auditorium) > 시간(during the lecture yesterday)' 순서가 가장 자연스럽습니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 어법상 잘못된 문장은?
choices: The medicine worked effectively on all patients.
She efficiently organized the conference materials.
The team cooperated successfully on the project.
He specifically requested additional information.
The results were significantly different from previous studies.
problems.explanation: 모든 문장에서 부사가 동사 또는 형용사를 올바르게 수식하고 있습니다. (문제 수정: 모든 문장이 올바름)

problems.instruction: 다음 중 어법상 올바른 문장은?
choices: Should you need any assistance, please contact us immediately.
Should you need any assistance, contact us please immediately.
You should need any assistance, please contact us immediately.
Should need you any assistance, please contact us immediately.
Should you any assistance need, please contact us immediately.
problems.explanation: 가정법 미래에서 If가 생략되면 주어와 조동사 should가 도치됩니다. 'If you should need...'가 'Should you need...'로 바뀐 올바른 문장입니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 대화에서 B의 답변 중 어법상 올바른 것은?
choices: "Yes, she did it."
"Yes, she did carefully."
"Yes, she did so."
"Yes, she completed."
"Yes, carefully she did."
problems.stem:
A: "Did she complete the assignment carefully?"
B: _______
problems.explanation: so는 앞서 언급된 동사구(complete the assignment carefully)를 대신하는 대부사로 사용될 수 있습니다. 'Yes, she did so.'는 'Yes, she completed the assignment carefully.'를 의미합니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 어법상 올바른 문장은?
choices: He suggested carefully reviewing the proposal.
He suggested reviewing carefully the proposal.
He carefully suggested reviewing the proposal.
He suggested reviewing the proposal carefully.
모든 문장이 올바름
problems.explanation: 부사는 동명사를 수식할 때 동명사 앞(①), 동명사구 뒤(④), 또는 본동사 앞(③)에 위치할 수 있습니다. 문맥상 모두 가능합니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 의미상 가장 적절한 문장은?
choices: The theory is very extremely important.
The theory is quite very important.
The theory is extremely important.
The theory is too very important.
The theory is so very important.
problems.explanation: very와 extremely는 모두 정도를 나타내는 강조부사로, 함께 사용하는 것은 어색하며 의미상 중복됩니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 어법상 올바른 문장은?
choices: The experiment was carefully conducted by experienced researchers.
The experiment was conducted carefully by experienced researchers.
Carefully the experiment was conducted by experienced researchers.
The experiment carefully was conducted by experienced researchers.
모든 문장이 올바름
problems.explanation: 수동태 문장에서 방법부사는 'be + p.p.' 사이(①), p.p. 뒤(②), 또는 문장 맨 앞(③)에 올 수 있습니다. 모두 문법적으로 가능합니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 어법상 올바른 문장은?
choices: The student who always studies hard will succeed.
The student who studies always hard will succeed.
The student who studies hard always will succeed.
The always student who studies hard will succeed.
The student always who studies hard will succeed.
problems.explanation: 관계대명사절 내에서도 빈도부사 always는 일반동사 studies 앞에 위치하는 것이 가장 자연스럽습니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 어법상 올바른 문장은?
choices: Fortunately, the weather improved before the outdoor experiment.
The weather, fortunately, improved before the outdoor experiment.
The weather improved, fortunately, before the outdoor experiment.
The weather improved before the outdoor experiment, fortunately.
모든 문장이 올바름
problems.explanation: Fortunately와 같은 문장부사는 문장 맨 앞, 주어와 동사 사이(콤마와 함께), 또는 문장 맨 뒤 등 다양한 위치에 올 수 있습니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글에서 어법상 잘못된 부분은?
choices: observed
closely
carefully
much
previously
problems.stem: "Never before had scientists ①<u>observed</u> such a phenomenon so ②<u>closely</u>. The team ③<u>carefully</u> analyzed the data and found that the results were ④<u>much</u> significant than ⑤<u>previously</u> thought."
problems.explanation: 비교급(significant than...)을 수식할 때는 very가 아닌 much를 사용해야 합니다. 하지만 여기서는 'significant'가 원급이므로 'much more significant'가 되거나, 문맥상 'very significant'가 되어야 합니다. 'much significant'는 틀린 표현입니다.
